name: Boeuf Bourguignonne
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds beef round, cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the wine, olive oil, salt, pepper, thyme, and bay leaf in a large bowl.
2. Slice one onion, one carrot, and one clove of garlic. Add these to the wine mixture.
3. Marinate the beef in this mixture for at least two hours, up to 24 hours, turning occasionally.
4. Remove the meat and pat it dry. Strain the marinade and reserve it.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons butter with olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Brown the meat quickly on all sides. Transfer the meat to a two-quart baking dish.
6. Deglaze the skillet with the reserved marinade and add to the baking dish.
7. In the skillet, melt 2 tablespoons butter and sauté the remaining onions, carrots, and garlic for about 5 minutes until lightly browned.
8. Add flour and stir for one minute. Then add the marinade, broth, and tomato paste. Stir until the mixture comes to a boil.
9. Pour the sauce over the meat and cover with foil. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 2-1/2 hours.
10. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in the skillet and sauté the white onions with salt and sugar until golden. Add mushrooms and sauté for two minutes more.
11. Return the white onions, mushrooms, and tomatoes to the beef and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
